abstract: California State Water Resources Control Board resolution adopting a regulatory definition of microplastics in drinking water. This document establishes the official state definition for microplastics as synthetic polymer particles that are less than or equal to 5 millimeters in length, insoluble in water, and persistent in the environment. The resolution provides the foundational regulatory framework for microplastics monitoring and management in California's drinking water systems.
Resolution No. 2020-0021: Adoption of Definition of 'Microplastics in Drinking Water'
Document establishes regulatory definition for microplastics in drinking water context
